hook.text: In 2010, Ethan Marcotte coined 'responsive web design.' One website that adapts to any screen size. Revolutionary.

buildup.text: Before responsive design, companies built separate mobile sites at m.example.com. Maintaining two sites was expensive.

discovery.text: CSS media queries let layouts reshape based on screen width. One codebase, infinite layouts. Elegant.

twist.text: Mobile traffic surpassed desktop in 2016. Responsive design went from 'nice to have' to 'absolute necessity.'

climax.text: Google made mobile-friendliness a search ranking factor. Non-responsive sites got pushed to page two.

punchline.text: One site, every screen. Responsive design became the only option.

quiz.question: What problem did responsive web design solve?
options: The need to maintain separate desktop and mobile websites for different screen sizes
Slow page load times on mobile networks
Browser compatibility issues between Chrome and Safari
